description:
You are assisting a capacity-planning engineer who keeps weekly CSV reports under:

/home/user/capacity/weekly_report.csv

Your job is to make a compressed copy of that file so it can be archived off-site.

1. Using a **single** terminal command line, create a gzip-compressed copy of the report named
   /home/user/capacity/weekly_report.csv.gz
   • The original CSV file MUST remain in place and unchanged.  
   • The new file must be a valid gzip archive that, when uncompressed, re-creates the exact original content byte-for-byte.

2. That same command line must also print the exact text

COMPRESSION DONE

to STDOUT as its final line.  
   • Nothing except that single line of text should be printed after the gzip operation finishes.

Automated grading will verify both:
• The existence and validity of /home/user/capacity/weekly_report.csv.gz  
• That COMPRESSION DONE was the very last line written to the console.
truth:
Initial environment:
• Directory /home/user/capacity already exists (permissions 755).
• File /home/user/capacity/weekly_report.csv exists with permissions 644 and the following exact 5-line content (including the final newline):

week,start,end,cpu_usage_pct,mem_usage_pct
23,2024-06-03,2024-06-09,57.3,62.1
24,2024-06-10,2024-06-16,59.8,63.7
25,2024-06-17,2024-06-23,61.2,65.4
26,2024-06-24,2024-06-30,60.0,64.0

Grader checks after agent action:
1. File /home/user/capacity/weekly_report.csv.gz exists.
2. gunzip -c /home/user/capacity/weekly_report.csv.gz produces byte-for-byte identical output to the original CSV (computed through sha256 checksums).
3. The last non-empty line of the combined STDOUT from the agent’s final command is exactly: COMPRESSION DONE
name: task_000000_5bac2c03
